text: Just a heads-up for everyone ordering chicken from Blinkit. Today I ordered a 450 g pack of Godrej boneless chicken. I usually don’t check the weight, but this time I was making a recipe that required exactly 450 g, so I placed it on my kitchen weighing scale. To my surprise, the pack weighed only 380 g, not 450 g. I contacted Blinkit, and they promptly refunded me for the shortfall, which I appreciate. However, that’s not really the point. Most customers don’t weigh their groceries every time they receive an order, so discrepancies like this can easily go unnoticed. My suggestion to everyone is to check the weight of chicken, fish, or any other items sold by weight whenever possible. Hopefully, this was just an isolated packing error, but it’s worth being aware of.

text: Hey Vibhanshu, First things first I love watching your videos. I was also analysing the Financial statements of Qcom companies because I am interested to see what lies in future for these Companies. First thing first, Instamart is bleeding right now because their Marketing spends, Capex (expansion) and may be inefficiency (Can't say for sure as they never clearly said in result that WE ARE INEFFICIENT THAN OUR PEERS) (other details as mentioned in their Financials) (but still blinkit is more popular in terms of brand awareness. Wtf is heading marketing for swiggy and spending so much money should be held accountable) Speaking of GOV and NOV, Blinkit sells wayy more High Ticket, High Priced, High margin products like iphones, headphones, Electronics etc. heck even 190 litre fridges. And electronics offer very high margins. And Since they are now shifting to inventory led model they are basically trying to become Dmart or Reliance for Online Grocery which gives them full quality control (which increases customer trust in long run resulting in higher orders) and also price control since they can have higher bargaining power while placing orders in Bulk. They will be keeping whole 100% of the margins instead of 20-30% like Instamart. Which overall impacts results a lot. Here's an example for Non Finance, Non Geek folks: Product A: COGS: 800 MRP: 2500 Discount: 30% (-750) Revenue recieved: 1750 Overheads, Delivery fees, Salary etc all consists of around say (48.5%) Remaining is 900. So keeping it simple lets assume 100 is Net profit here so Swiggy, since it runs on commission based model (market place model will keep 30-40% of revenue at max, say 40% here to maximise our result) NP: 40, Blinkit since it is wholesaler, relailer, Platform etc etc. It will take everything 100 (Plus platform fees and all but let's keep things simple) Hence Blinkit will anyday have much much more NP than Instamart. (Assuming both are operating at same efficiency). It's the same case of Dmart and XYZ mall where all brand outlets are independent shops. Now coming to App. Here, I respectfully disagree with you. I think only reason Instamart is able to Achieve whatever it has is because of it being integrated in main Swiggy app. I use Instamart because it let's me order from one app and I do not like installing 100 apps on phone so whenever I need food or grocery I just go to Swiggy app (They also launched separate Instamart app BTW). They also now have improved the UI, UX a lot, giving you options in big and bold like Food, Dineout, Instamart Genie etc. (They offer separate app for each btw). Brand Color and Brand image is something that they need to work and yes Sometimes their app just doesn't feels as polished as Blinkit. (But I do not like blinkit either in terms of smoothness. I think in that area Big Basket is the best). So if you read this far, (Just Say BINGO if you did) Congratulations you got nothing you didn't already know. But I just tried sharing my view that 80% of issue lies in their business model which isn't sustainable at all (Even worse model that theirs was of Dunzo which failed). And Remaining 20% lies in marketing and efficiency (IDK where are they spending their million dollar Marketing Budget) One out of context thing but I generally order from Swiggy because their platform allows ordering from PC while Zomato is Stupid enough to not let me do so. Now what's the solution according to Some World Renowned Harvard Prodigy whom you've never head of: 1. Shift towards Inventory led Model. 2. Better prediction models. This will reduce wastage of Perishables. 3. Start analysing Searches and work on them. (Suppose if 100,000 people searched iphone 17 on Swiggy then just make it available) 4. Start Selling High Margin, High Ticket items (Electronics, Apparels etc) Basically moving from necessity to Luxury products as people are ready to pay more there and margins are thicker too. 5. Become Reliance Retail or Dmart or Big Bazaar (Inhouse Tiered brands with Packaging and Branding getting better with price Say Golden harvest almonds vs Karmiq almonds both are almonds but with 30% difference in price and may be 10% better quality but Packaging and Branding gets much premium.) So yeah these were my 2 paise (2 cents). If anyone from company is reading this and want to implement then please do. I love ordering things all from 1 app. Thanks for Reading! Shubhanshu

text: As an ex-employee, I can tell that the Blinkit CEO has openly said that they are not going after the freeloaders or the customers who are always looking for discounts; Rivals can keep such customers coz such customers are so price-conscious that they don't contribute much to the actual margin (by sticking to the list of items & prices and avoiding impulsive purchases). Coming to Swiggy's own brand scheme, Blinkit already tested the private label strategy when it operated as "Grofers" 6 years back, and slowly phased it out until 2024. Because, running your own label properly requires a significant infra, capital, research & QC setup and even then your product will at most be a decent product, and there's no guarantee! Introducing own label (& your own production unit) is not a very good strategy as now you are competing with another market segment with big players like ITC, HUL & Reckitt and smaller players, both of whom have more expertise as they are focused on a single problem, while Swiggy now has to focus on 2 problems: quick commerce delivery as well as private label manufacturing.

meta.relevance_reason: Explains how discount-seeking behavior and private label/QC strategy impact customer retention and margins.
